reforms.description: The authorities have initiated a substantial six-year plan aimed at reforming tertiary education, which includes increasing staff recruitment, raising the population of Arab and Ultra-orthodox students, and boosting spending on competitive research funds. Additionally, business support for innovation has been ramped up, including the doubling of the budget of the Israel Science Foundation and the establishment of a programme for developing alternative fuels.
reforms.package_name: Tertiary Education and Innovation Support Reform
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The increase in funding for research and education is expected to enhance innovation capacity and human capital, leading to higher productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The budget for the Israel Science Foundation has been doubled and the programme for alternative fuels has been established.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in the funding and support for research and education.
reforms.source_quote: This latest initiative comprises a substantial six-year plan whose main goals are to increase staff recruitment, raise the population of Arab and Ultra-orthodox students, boost spending on competitive research funds, upgrade teaching and research infrastructure and reform budgeting.

reforms.description: The new fiscal regime for natural gas and oil resources significantly alters the tax and royalty structure, aiming to increase government revenues from resource extraction while ensuring that the benefits are shared across generations through a proposed sovereign wealth fund.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform is designed to enhance government revenues, which can be reinvested into public goods and services, potentially supporting innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The new fiscal regime has been established and is in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a major overhaul of the fiscal framework governing natural resources.
reforms.source_quote: The legislation was passed by the Knesset in April 2011 with only minor concessions made with respect to the original proposal.

reforms.description: The government plans to maintain the green credit system while reducing the basic rate of purchase tax on vehicles, which is currently set at 83%. This reform aims to encourage the purchase of low-emission vehicles and improve environmental outcomes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging low-emission vehicle purchases can lead to environmental benefits and stimulate innovation in the automotive sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans to maintain and adjust the green credit system.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, this reform is not as comprehensive as major structural changes.
reforms.source_quote: Maintain the green credit system, but reduce the basic rate of purchase tax (currently 83%).

reforms.description: The government is exploring additional regulations and public-information campaigns to promote efficiency savings in vehicle use, which may include mandatory standards and efficiency certifications.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The impact of these measures on innovation and efficiency is uncertain at this stage.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is in the exploratory phase regarding these regulations.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is more of an exploratory nature rather than a significant policy change.
reforms.source_quote: Consider additional regulation and public-information campaigns to promote efficiency savings in vehicle use.
